attempts.error:
Predicate argument has wrong type:
  atLocation rack coffee_maker: rack is not a agent
  atLocation knife coffee_maker: knife is not a agent
  atLocation mug coffee_maker: mug is not a agent
  atLocation fridge coffee_maker: fridge is not a agent
attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Chill the mug in the fridge and set it on the rack.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains the following objects relevant to the domain and instruction:

1. **Agent**: A robotic agent is present, initially not at any object's location. It is capable of moving, picking up, and manipulating objects.

2. **Receptacles**:
   - **Fridge**: A dark gray refrigerator with a digital display panel showing temperature settings for “Fridge” (5°C) and “Freezer” (-15°C). It has an ice/water dispenser on the door. The fridge is openable and currently closed. It is positioned against a tiled wall and floor, to the right of the countertop area.
   - **Rack**: A three-tiered wooden shelving unit with dark brown finish. It is not openable but functions as a receptacle for placing objects. The top shelf holds two gray bottles with dark caps and a small clear bottle; the middle shelf holds a dark gray plate and a salt shaker; the bottom shelf is empty. The rack is against a maroon wall with a tiled floor beneath.

3. **Objects**:
   - **Mug**: A white ceramic mug, currently sitting on the drip tray of a black coffee maker on the countertop. It is not currently held, cleaned, heated, cooled, or sliced. It is the target object for chilling and placement.
   - **Coffee Maker**: A black appliance with a white mug on its tray. It is not a receptacle and not relevant to the domain predicates beyond being a surface where the mug rests.
   - **Toaster**: A silver two-slot toaster on the countertop to the right of the coffee maker. It is not a receptacle and not relevant to the task.
   - **Knife**: A silver knife lying on the countertop to the right of the toaster. It is an object of type “knife” and is currently not held or used.

4. **Spatial Relationships**:
   - The mug is on the coffee maker, which is on the countertop.
   - The toaster and knife are also on the same countertop, to the right of the coffee maker.
   - The fridge is to the right of the countertop area, standing on the tiled floor.
   - The rack is in a separate area, against a maroon wall, with the middle shelf holding a plate and shaker, and the bottom shelf empty and available for placing the mug.

5. **Current States**:
   - The mug is not chilled, not held, not sliced, not cleaned, not heated, and not toggled.
   - The fridge is closed and not opened.
   - The rack is not openable and is currently holding other objects but has available space (bottom shelf).
   - The knife is on the countertop and not held.
   - The agent is not at any object’s location initially.

6. **Visual Properties**:
   - The mug is white and cylindrical with a handle.
   - The fridge is dark gray with a glossy finish and a digital control panel.
   - The rack is dark brown wood with visible grain and metal corner brackets.
   - The knife is metallic with a straight blade and no visible handle details.
   - The countertop is light brown with a textured surface, and the floor is beige tile.

The task requires the agent to first go to the mug, pick it up, go to the fridge, open it, place the mug inside to chill, close the fridge, retrieve the chilled mug, go to the rack, and place the mug on the bottom shelf.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem chill_mug_and_place_on_rack)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        mug - object
        fridge - fridge
        rack - receptacle
        coffee_maker - object
        knife - knife
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 mug))
        (not (atLocation agent1 fridge))
        (not (atLocation agent1 rack))
        (not (atLocation agent1 coffee_maker))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 mug))
        (not (isCool mug))
        (not (isHot mug))
        (not (isClean mug))
        (not (isSliced mug))
        (not (isOn mug))
        (not (isToggled mug))
        (not (opened fridge))
        (openable fridge)
        (inReceptacle mug coffee_maker)
        (atLocation mug coffee_maker)
        (atLocation knife coffee_maker)
        (atLocation fridge coffee_maker)
        (atLocation rack coffee_maker)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle mug rack)
        (isCool mug)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem chill_mug_and_place_on_rack)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        mug - object
        fridge - fridge
        rack - receptacle
        coffee_maker - object
        knife - knife
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 mug))
        (not (atLocation agent1 fridge))
        (not (atLocation agent1 rack))
        (not (atLocation agent1 coffee_maker))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 mug))
        (not (isCool mug))
        (not (isHot mug))
        (not (isClean mug))
        (not (isSliced mug))
        (not (isOn mug))
        (not (isToggled mug))
        (not (opened fridge))
        (openable fridge)
        (inReceptacle mug coffee_maker)
        (atLocation mug coffee_maker)
        (atLocation knife coffee_maker)
        (atLocation fridge coffee_maker)
        (atLocation rack coffee_maker)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle mug rack)
        (isCool mug)
    ))
)

    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Chill the mug in the fridge and set it on the rack.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains a kitchen environment with several objects relevant to the task. The primary objects of interest are a white ceramic mug, a black coffee maker, a silver toaster, a dark grey refrigerator, and a dark brown wooden three-tiered shelving unit (rack).

1.  **Objects and their types:**
    *   **Receptacles:** The refrigerator is a receptacle, as it can hold items and is shown with a digital control panel and an ice/water dispenser, indicating it is openable. The wooden shelving unit is also a receptacle, as it holds various items on its shelves.
    *   **Objects:** The white mug, the black coffee maker, the silver toaster, and the wooden shelving unit are all classified as objects. The coffee maker and toaster are appliances, while the mug is a container. The shelving unit is a multi-level receptacle.

2.  **Quantities:**
    *   There is one white mug.
    *   There is one black coffee maker.
    *   There is one silver toaster.
    *   There is one dark grey refrigerator.
    *   There is one dark brown wooden shelving unit (rack).

3.  **Spatial relationships:**
    *   The white mug is initially placed on the drip tray of the black coffee maker, which is situated on a light brown countertop.
    *   The silver toaster is positioned on the same countertop, to the right of the coffee maker.
    *   The refrigerator is located on the right side of the scene, with its front panel facing the viewer. The digital display shows the freezer temperature at -15°C and the fridge temperature at 5°C.
    *   The wooden shelving unit (rack) is positioned against a wall with a red lower half and a beige upper half. It is not on the countertop but on the floor, to the right of the refrigerator.
    *   The mug is not currently in the refrigerator or on the rack. The refrigerator is not open. The rack is empty on its bottom shelf, has a plate and a salt shaker on the middle shelf, and has two bottles and a small container on the top shelf.

4.  **Current state of objects:**
    *   The mug is not currently held by any agent, is not in any receptacle, and is not chilled or heated.
    *   The refrigerator is not opened.
    *   The shelving unit (rack) is not being interacted with.
    *   The coffee maker and toaster are on the countertop, but their operational state (on/off) is not specified in the images.

5.  **Colors and positions:**
    *   The mug is white and is located on the black coffee maker.
    *   The coffee maker is black and is on the left side of the countertop.
    *   The toaster is silver and is on the right side of the countertop.
    *   The refrigerator is dark grey and is on the right side of the scene.
    *   The shelving unit (rack) is dark brown and is on the floor against a wall with a red and beige color scheme. It is positioned to the right of the refrigerator.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem chill_mug_and_set_on_rack)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        mug - object
        coffee_maker - object
        toaster - object
        fridge - fridge
        rack - receptacle
        countertop - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 countertop)
        (inReceptacle mug coffee_maker)
        (openable fridge)
        (not (opened fridge))
        (not (isCool mug))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle mug rack)
        (isCool mug)
    ))
)
```

(define (problem chill_mug_and_set_on_rack)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        mug - object
        coffee_maker - object
        toaster - object
        fridge - fridge
        rack - receptacle
        countertop - object
    )
    (:init

        (inReceptacle mug coffee_maker)

    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle mug rack)
        (isCool mug)
    ))
)

(define (problem chill_mug_and_set_on_rack)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        mug - object
        coffee_maker - object
        toaster - object
        fridge - fridge
        rack - receptacle
        countertop - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 countertop)
        (inReceptacle mug coffee_maker)
        (openable fridge)
        (not (opened fridge))
        (not (isCool mug))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle mug rack)
        (isCool mug)
    ))
)
